item.title: French group Veolia aims $1.2 bln in revenue from data centres, chips by 2030
item.description: French utility Veolia aims to almost double its revenue вҖӢfrom providing energy and water вҖҢmanagement solutions to data centres and chipmakers by 2030, it said on Tuesday, вҖӢbetting on strong demand from вҖӢboth sectors.

item.title: US solar and wind contract prices rose sharply in first quarter
item.description: U.S. solar and wind contract prices rose sharply in the first quarter вҖӢas project developers faced tariffs, labor shortages and вҖҢpermitting challenges amid soaring demand for power from data centers, according to a report published вҖӢon Tuesday.

item.title: IMF expects pace of Japan's interest rate hikes to speed up slightly
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said on Tuesday it expected the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates gradually, but at a вҖӢslightly faster pace than projected six months ago.

item.title: Long Iran war may require painful central bank tightening, IMF chief economist says
item.description: Central banks may need вҖҢto inflict much more economic pain to control inflation fueled by a long Middle East war than they did to control the spike in prices after the pandemic, the International Monetary Fund's chief economist said.

item.title: Banks press for more capital relief, make plans to use excess capital
item.description: As the largest banks expect to release billions in вҖӢcapital with regulatory reform, many are вҖҢalready making plans about how to spend them.

item.title: Global satellite internet companies at a glance
item.description: Amazon.com said on Tuesday it would buy satellite company Globalstar in an $11.57 billion deal, as вҖӢit looks to take on bigger вҖҢrival SpaceX's Starlink.

item.title: US producer prices increase less than expected in March
item.description: U.S. producer prices increased less than expected in March as the cost of services was unchanged, but surging energy prices вҖӢbecause of the war with Iran were fanning inflation pressures.

item.title: PepsiCo faces pressure to show Elliott-triggered turnaround is working
item.description: Seven months after activist investor Elliott disclosed a $4 billion stake in PepsiCo, the U.S. вҖӢbeverages giant is under pressure to show a turnaround drive including price cuts and brand relaunches can deliver the volume growth investors вҖҢcrave.

item.title: Amazon signs $11.57 billion deal for satellite firm Globalstar to challenge Musk's Starlink
item.description: Amazon.com said on Tuesday it would acquire Globalstar in an $11.57 billion deal, bolstering its fledgling satellite business as it looks to take вҖӢon Elon Musk-led bigger rival Starlink.

item.title: Citi profit jumps 42% as market volatility lifts trading revenue
item.description: Citigroup's first-quarter profit rose 42% as geopolitical tensions fueled market volatility and trading revenue, while strong dealmaking buoyed investment banking fees.

item.title: CarMax posts quarterly loss on goodwill charge, weak used-car demand
item.description: Used car retailer CarMax reported a fourth-quarter loss on Tuesday, hurt by a goodwill impairment charge and вҖӢshrinking margins from used vehicles.

item.title: Grocer Albertsons forecasts annual sales below estimates
item.description: Albertsons forecast its annual sales вҖӢbelow Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, pointing вҖҢto pressure on demand from fierce competition with bigger rivals rolling out heavier discounts.

item.title: ASML investors bet on 'picks and shovels' of AI revolution
item.description: Investors in , EuropeвҖҷs most valuable listed company, are betting the chip-equipment maker can raise financial forecasts when it reports вҖҢfirst-quarter earnings on Wednesday, as demand for artificial intelligence chips keeps orders for its machines running flat out.

item.title: China orders safety checks on smart vehicle road tests after robotaxi incident
item.description: China has ordered local authorities вҖӢto conduct self-inspections and вҖҢstrengthen safety oversight of road tests involving intelligent вҖӢconnected vehicles following вҖӢa recent incident.

item.title: BetMGM cuts 2026 revenue forecast on weak US sports betting
item.description: U.S. online betting operator вҖӢBetMGM cut вҖҢits 2026 revenue outlook on вҖӢTuesday, citing вҖӢa soft first-quarter for вҒ its online вҖӢsports betting вҖӢbusiness.

item.title: EV maker Lucid names Silvio Napoli as CEO, shares rise
item.description: Lucid on Tuesday вҖҢnamed Silvio Napoli as the company's вҖӢnext chief вҖӢexecutive officer, as it вҒ looks to ramp вҖӢup production and вҖӢsales of its electric vehicles amid a вҖӢhighly competitive EV вҖӢenvironment.

item.title: Bond investors target steeper US yield curve on bets for slower growth, more debt issuance
item.description: Bond investors are doubling down on so-called "curve steepener" trades that are bullish on short-dated U.S. Treasuries but bearish on the long end, reflecting expectations that the Federal Reserve will eventually resume cutting interest rates.

item.title: Morning Bid: War-weary markets filter out noise
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Investors size up landmark new chapter for Hungary post-Orban
item.description: Investors are banking on a positive new chapter for Hungary as incoming Prime Minister Peter Magyar insists there is no time to waste following his resounding defeat of Viktor Orban - provided he can stick to his plans.

item.title: Industry group cuts global steel demand forecast for 2026 due to Iran war
item.description: The World Steel Association on Tuesday cut its вҖӢforecast for global crude steel demand this year, вҖҢpartly due to the Iran war slashing consumption in the Middle East.

item.title: Dow names insider Karen Carter CEO as Fitterling moves to executive chair role
item.description: Chemicals maker Dow named operations chief Karen Carter as its new CEO on Tuesday, handing the вҖӢreins to a long-time insider as the company grapples with вҖҢweak demand, restructuring and a sweeping overhaul of its global footprint.

item.title: JPMorgan profit gets a boost from trading, dealmaking
item.description: JPMorgan Chase reported a 13% rise in first-quarter profit on Tuesday, as record gains in trading due to volatility in markets and a pickup вҖӢin dealmaking lifted results.

item.title: Wells Fargo profit rises on interest income, trading boost
item.description: Wells Fargo's profit rose in the first quarter, aided by higher income from interest payments and increased trading gains from volatile markets.

item.title: Deutsche Boerse acquires a $200 mln stake in Kraken
item.description: German exchange operator вҖӢDeutsche Boerse said вҖҢon Tuesday that it вҖӢhad acquired вҖӢa 200 million dollar вҒ stake вҖӢin US-based cryptocurrency вҖӢexchange Kraken.

item.title: J&J's first-quarter profit beats estimates even as Stelara sales disappoint
item.description: Johnson & Johnson reported first-quarter earnings that beat вҖӢWall Street expectations on Tuesday and raised its full-year forecast, as strong demand for cancer вҖҢdrug Darzalex and psoriasis treatment Tremfya more than offset a steep falloff in sales of its blockbuster autoimmune drug Stelara.

item.title: BlackRock quarterly profit rises on active ETFs, performance fees
item.description: BlackRock reported a rise in first-quarter profit on Tuesday, as the asset вҖӢmanager saw strong inflows into its exchange traded funds and вҖҢearned more from performance fees, sending its shares up 2.8% in trading before the bell.

item.title: US truckers' diesel spend hits record high on Middle East conflict
item.description: The more than 3 million U.S. truckers who move goods for everything from grocery stores to factories to construction sites are grappling with the highest diesel prices in years, compounding pressure on the industry as Iran war-related вҖӢoil price spikes also threaten to weaken freight demand.

item.title: US small business sentiment falls to 11-month low in March
item.description: U.S. small-business sentiment dropped to an 11-month low in March as a surge in oil prices because of the вҖӢwar in Iran overshadowed the benefits from low taxes, a вҖҢsurvey showed.

item.title: Fed nominee Warsh files financial disclosure in step towards confirmation
item.description: Kevin Warsh, the former Federal Reserve governor chosen вҖӢby President Donald Trump to run the central вҖҢbank, has submitted financial disclosures that are required for his nomination to advance through the Senate, beginning with a вҖӢyet-to-be-scheduled hearing.

item.title: Kremlin says internet curbs are temporary, will be lifted once security threat passes
item.description: The Kremlin said on Tuesday that internet restrictions, вҖӢwhich it conceded had caused disruption to many Russians, вҖҢwere needed for security reasons, but that they were temporary in nature and would be lifted once it was вҖӢsafe to do so.
